meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop starting and never finishing. Learn how portfolio-driven development helps founders ship MVPs faster using Antigravity and Claude Code strategies.
content.answer_block: To overcome project stagnation, shift from building 'features' to building 'portfolio assets.' By scoping projects as discrete, shippable units, you create technical leverage. Use Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to automate implementation, ensuring you hit milestones quickly and maintain the momentum required to secure your first paying customers.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 90% finished projects. You have the vision, the code, and the drive, yet you find yourself trapped in the 'infinite iteration' loop, never actually deploying a product that captures value. This isn't a failure of skill; it is a failure of system design.

True founders don't just build code; they build assets. By adopting a portfolio-first mindset, you transform every stalled project into a modular building block. This approach forces you to ship, validates your technical architecture, and creates a repository of reusable components that shorten your time-to-market for every future venture.

steps.title: Define the High-Value MVP Scope
steps.description: Identify the one core feature that solves a burning problem. Use this to create a project scope that is finishable in under 10 days.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Evaluate this SaaS idea: [Insert Idea]. Break it down into a 10-day MVP scope focused exclusively on the primary value proposition. Ignore secondary features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review this scope and generate a folder structure for a modular Next.js MVP that supports future expansion.'

steps.title: Architect for Reusability
steps.description: Structure your code so components act as micro-services. This ensures your project is a portfolio piece, not a monolith.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a directory structure that separates core business logic from UI components to ensure high modularity and portability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a shared components library folder and move the base auth logic into a reusable service file.'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate with AI
steps.description: Stop writing boilerplate. Use AI to generate the heavy lifting so you can focus on the business logic that differentiates your product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the boilerplate for a secure Supabase-integrated user authentication flow with role-based access control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install @supabase/supabase-js' && claude ask 'Implement the auth service using the generated boilerplate.'

steps.title: Implement Continuous Deployment
steps.description: Automate your shipping pipeline. Every push must be a deployable version of your portfolio asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deployment to Vercel on every merge to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the .github/workflows/deploy.yml file and configure it for a standard production build.'

steps.title: Finalize and Document for Impact
steps.description: A project without documentation is just code. Write a technical case study inside your repo to sell your expertise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for this project that highlights the technical challenges solved and the business value delivered.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Summarize the technical decisions made in this project into a concise case study format for my portfolio.'

pro_tips: Never build custom auth; use Clerk or Supabase to reclaim 20 hours of development time.
Adopt the '80/20 Rule'—ship the 20% of features that provide 80% of the user value, then stop.
Use Claude Code to perform 'automated code reviews' on your own PRs to catch architectural debt before it scales.
Maintain a 'Component Registry' folder in every project so you can copy-paste logic into the next one.
Treat your GitHub commit history as a resume; clear, atomic commits build trust with future investors.
content.next_steps: Once your project is deployed, share it on LinkedIn or X with a link to your repo. Use the 'Case Study' README generated in step 5 to demonstrate your engineering authority and attract potential leads or partners.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting great ideas die in private repositories. Build, ship, and scale your way to a profitable SaaS portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
